data.title: First Merchants enters into a definitive agreement to acquire First Savings Financial
data.description:
• First Merchants (NASDAQ:FRME) on Thursday announced that it has entered into a definitive merger agreement to acquire First Savings Financial Group(FSFG) in an all-stock transaction valued at approximately $241.3 million.
  • Immediately following the merger, First Savings Bank will merge with and into First Merchants Bank.
  • Under the terms, First Savings shareholders will receive 0.85 shares of First Merchants stock for each share of First Savings they own.
  • The transaction, which is expected to close in Q1 2026, will create a combined entity with $21 billion in assets and 127 branches across Indiana, Michigan, and Ohio.
  • First Savings, based in Jeffersonville, operates 16 branches in southern Indiana and has total assets of $2.4 billion. The merger is projected to be accretive to earnings per share by 11% in 2027.
  • FRME -0.68% premarket to $39.26.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Vesta prices $500 million offering of senior notes due 2033
data.description:
• Corporación Inmobiliaria Vesta (VESTA) announced on Thursday that it has priced an offering of $500 million aggregate principal amount of 5.500% senior unsecured notes due January 30, 2033.
  •  The notes will be guaranteed on a senior unsecured basis by certain of Vesta’s subsidiaries.
  • Vesta intends to use the net proceeds from the notes offering to repay certain indebtedness, and for capital expenditure and other general corporate purposes.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Equinix launches new products - Distributed AI infrastructure, others
data.description:
Equinix (NASDAQ:EQIX) announced on Thursday the launch of its Distributed AI infrastructure product.

The infrastructure is expected to help companies support use cases like real-time decision-making for predictive maintenance in manufacturing, dynamic retail optimization, and faster fraud detection in financial services.

Other key launches included the Fabric Intelligence, a software layer, and the AI Solutions Lab at Equinix Solution Validation Center facilities across 20 locations in 10 countries. 

The data center REIT said these products, which were unveiled at the inaugural AI Summit, are expected to become available in the first quarter of 2026.

The summit also discussed an expansion of Equinix's AI ecosystem.

data.title: Atlantic International's lands contract with global e-commerce company
data.description:
• Atlantic International (NASDAQ:ATLN) on Thursday said that its subsidiary Lyneer Staffing Solutions has secured a contract with the potential to generate up to $17 million in revenue over the next 12 months.
  • Aims to provide comprehensive staffing services for a global e-commerce logistics company across multiple states.
  • The partnership encompasses both temporary staffing for peak demand periods and direct-hire placements for permanent roles.
  • ATLN closed -8.09% at $4.2.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Predictive Oncology announces 1-for-15 reverse stock split
data.description:
• Predictive Oncology (NASDAQ:POAI) on Thursday said it will effect a 1-for-15 reverse stock split of its issued and outstanding shares of common stock.
  • Predictive Oncology’s common stock will continue to be traded on Nasdaq under the symbol POAI and will begin trading on a reverse split-adjusted basis when the market opens on September 30, 2025. 
  • The reverse stock split is primarily intended to bring the company into compliance with the minimum bid price requirement to maintain the listing of the company’s common stock on Nasdaq.
  • Prior to the reverse stock split, the company had 11.43M shares of its common stock outstanding and immediately following the reverse stock split, there will be 762,414 shares of its common stock outstanding.
  • POAI +0.84% premarket to $1.19.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Questor announces award of $9M contract
data.description:
• Questor Technology (TSXV:QST:CA) on Thursday said it has secured a $9 million contract to supply clean combustion solutions to a large energy services provider in Mexico for rental over three years.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Accenture slips as it expects growth to slow on federal spending cuts
data.description:
Accenture (NYSE:ACN) was in focus on Thursday as the consulting firm reported better-than-expected quarterly results, but said it expects growth to slow amid U.S. federal spending cuts.

Shares initially rose following the results, but fell 2.4% in premarket trading. 

Looking to fiscal 2026, Accenture expects to earn between $13.52 and $13.90 per share on an adjusted basis, with the midpoint of $13.71 slightly below the $13.78 per share estimate.

Revenue is expected to grow between 2% and 5% in local currency, however that excludes an impact of 1% to 1.5% from the U.S. federal business, Accenture said. Analysts had forecast 5.28% year-over-year revenue growth, or $18.52B.

The guidance comes as Accenture reported stronger-than-expected results for the fourth-quarter, aided by generative artificial intelligence. Generative AI new bookings in the quarter were $1.8B and $5.9B for the full-year. That helped the company earn $3.03 per share on an adjusted basis, better than the $2.99 per share estimate. Revenue rose 7.3% year-over-year to $17.6B, above the $17.46B estimate.

Adjusted operating margin during the period rose 10 basis points to 15.1%, while free cash flow for the period was $3.8B. 

“I am very pleased with our 7% growth in fiscal 2025, demonstrating our unique ability to deliver for our clients as they seek our help to reinvent and lead with AI,” Accenture Chair and CEO Julie Sweet said in a statement. “As clients continue to embrace reinvention to create value and drive financial results and business outcomes, they need help to build their digital core, prepare data and reimagine processes, all while training their people to work in entirely new ways. This is what Accenture does best and our strong results this year clearly illustrate our impact.”

data.title: EagleNXT receives order for 12 eBee TAC drones from Tough Stump Technologies
data.description:
• AgEagle Aerial Systems (NYSE:UAVS) on Thursday announced the receipt of an order for 12 eBee TAC tactical mapping drones from Tough Stump Technologies.
  • "The systems will be integrated into Tough Stump’s ARTEMIS kits, enhancing capabilities for rapid aerial reconnaissance, situational awareness, and intelligence gathering across defense, public safety, and humanitarian missions," the company said.
  • UAVS -3.73% premarket to $2.06.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Opendoor stock gains as Jane Street discloses 5.9% stake
data.description:
• Opendoor Technologies (NASDAQ:OPEN) shares popped over 4% Thursday morning on a disclosure that trading firm Jane Street has taken a 5.9% passive stake in the online real estate company.
  • Jane Street Group disclosed in a 13G filing that it collectively holds 44,031,310.18 shares, which represent 5.9% of Opendoor's total outstanding common stock.
  • Opendoor (NASDAQ:OPEN) shares surged 16.24% on Wednesday following a strong new home sales report that lifted investor sentiment. U.S. new home sales jumped 20.5% to 800K in August from 664K in July (revised from 652K), compared with the 650K consensus, according to data released by the U.S. Census Bureau on Wednesday.
  • The stock, which is up 414.3% YTD, received an additional lift in Wednesday’s after-hours trading after Jane Street disclosed its stake.

data.title: Investors ask Disney to share records related to Jimmy Kimmel's brief suspension
data.description:
Shareholder groups of media and entertainment giant Disney (NYSE:DIS) have raised questions regarding the company's decision to temporarily suspend Jimmy Kimmel's show on ABC.

A letter from lawyers who represent the American Federation of Teachers, Reporters Without Borders, Inc., and others asked Disney to send them records related to Kimmel’s suspension to get clarity on the matter.

Kimmel was taken off the network for six days for his remarks on slain right-wing political activist Charlie Kirk. The move resulted in a public outcry by advocates from both sides of the political aisle and triggered a wave of subscription cancellations by users who believe the company participated in curbing free speech. 

The letter condemned the company's decision against the comedian and said investors had the right to investigate whether Disney executives failed to fulfill their fiduciary duties.

"Although we are pleased that ABC did the right thing and put Jimmy Kimmel back on the air last night, due to the Trump administration’s continued threats to free speech, including with respect to ABC, we are writing to seek transparency into the initial decision to suspend him and his show," according to the letter sent on Wednesday.

"There is a credible basis to suspect that the Board and executives may have breached their fiduciary duties of loyalty, care, and good faith by placing improper political or affiliate considerations above the best interests of the Company and its stockholders."

The letter also indicated that if Disney did not send them the records related to Kimmel’s suspension in five business days, they would sue them.
